title: Research Analyst
raw_jd:
**About Us:**  

This is your chance to kickstart your career at HarrisX, at HarrisX, a leading technology, market research, and consulting company that blends corporate and public opinion research to uncover what drives people, brands, and policy. We specialize in understanding the connected consumer, running some of the most comprehensive studies on technology, telecom, and media behavior globally, while also serving as one of the most accurate pollsters in recent U.S. elections.
  
  

Our research combines quantitative and qualitative methods, behavioral data, and social analytics to deliver actionable insights to clients ranging from Fortune 100 corporations to media organizations, advocacy groups, and policymakers. Based in Washington DC and with offices in New York, Toronto, London, Los Angeles, Australia, and Singapore, HarrisX is a member of Stagwell Global.
  
  

**Our mission is simple:** To connect all the dots for our clients, in what we call “Connected Intelligence.”
  
  

**Overview:**  

As a Research Analyst on the Custom Research team at HarrisX, you will support end\-to\-end research engagements across corporate, media, and public opinion research projects. Working under the direction of a Research Manager, you will contribute to research design, build and program surveys, analyze data, and help develop client\-ready deliverables.
  
  

This is a hands\-on, execution\-focused role suited for someone early in their research career who is eager to develop technical depth across quantitative and qualitative methods. You will work as part of a collaborative project team alongside Research Managers, Directors, VPs, data scientists, and research operations team, contributing to studies that inform both corporate strategy and public narratives.
  
  

HarrisX offers strong growth opportunities for motivated analysts who are looking to build skills quickly and take on greater responsibility over time.
  
  

**Responsibilities :** \- Support the planning and execution of custom research projects, including timeline tracking, fieldwork coordination, and vendor management under the direction of a Research Manager. \- Assist in the development of research instruments, including survey questionnaires and moderator guides, in line with established methodological standards. \- Program surveys using platforms such as Decipher, Qualtrics, or ConfirmIT, and manage quality checks throughout the fieldwork process. \- Clean, process, and analyze survey data using Q, SPSS, or equivalent statistical tools, applying appropriate weighting and validation techniques. \- Contribute to the preparation of reports, presentations, and dashboards that translate findings into clear, actionable insights for client delivery. \- Support the integration of multiple data sources, including survey, behavioral, and secondary data, as directed by senior team members. \- Participate in client communications, documenting business objectives and project requirements under the guidance of a Research Manager or Director. \- Collaborate cross\-functionally with analysts, data scientists, and research operations colleagues across project workstreams. \- Apply and develop familiarity with AI\-powered tools used for survey automation, data synthesis, and content analysis as part of the team's evolving workflow. Qualifications: \- You have 1–2 years of experience in custom market research, data analysis, or a related research environment. \- You have academic and practical exposure to a range of research methodologies, including new product, brand, advertising, and customer satisfaction research. \- You are proficient in Excel and PowerPoint, and have working knowledge of Q, SPSS, SAS, or equivalent statistical software. Experience with R, Python, or data visualization tools is a plus. \- You have hands\-on experience programming surveys in platforms such as Decipher, Qualtrics, or ConfirmIT. \- You understand survey methodology fundamentals, including best practices for questionnaire design, statistical weighting, and data validation. \- You are comfortable working with multiple data types and have a foundation in data cleaning, analysis, and visualization. \- You communicate clearly in both written and verbal form, and can present findings in a structured, accessible way. \- You are detail\-oriented, able to manage competing priorities under tight deadlines, and comfortable operating with some ambiguity. \- You are curious about AI\-powered tools and open to applying them within research workflows, including survey automation and data synthesis. \- You hold a Bachelor's degree, preferably in economics, statistics, mathematics, political science, communications, or a related field. A Master's or advanced degree is a plus. Equal Opportunities:
  
  

HarrisX is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace. We welcome applicants of all backgrounds and do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.
  
  

**Compensation:** The base salary range for this position is $60,000–$70,000 annually, commensurate with experience.
